error_signature: cursor for text input can't be set to pointer
title: Text input cursor not changing to pointer
symptom: The cursor for a read-only text input remains as the default text cursor instead of changing to a pointer as intended.
applies_when: Text input is set to read-only
Cursor style is attempted to be changed to pointer
not_this_if: Input is editable or not set to read-only
root_cause: Read-only text inputs typically default to a text cursor, and CSS changes may not apply directly to them. Wrapping the input in a div allows for overriding the cursor style effectively.
fix_steps: Wrap the read-only text input in a div element.
Set the desired cursor style (e.g., pointer) on the div.
Apply custom CSS to disable pointer events on the input itself.
agent_instruction: Wrap the text input in a div and set the cursor style on the div.
